channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Palestinian teenager who died in Israeli prison showed signs of starvation, medical report says
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Starvation was likely the leading cause of death for a Palestinian teenager who died in an Israeli prison, according to an Israeli doctor who observed the autopsy.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia’s foothold in Syria presents conundrum for Trump
item.description: The Trump administration is torn over how to respond to Russia’s military presence in Syria and whether to ask the new transitional government to oust Russian forces from a naval and air base in the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Forecast of a week of rain adds to woes faced by victims of Myanmar quake, as death toll tops 3,500
item.description: People in Myanmar’s earthquake-stricken areas were braced for thunderstorms late Sunday, after heavy rains and winds the previous night disrupted rescue and relief operations and added to the misery of the many who lost their homes in the disaster and were forced to sleep in the open.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope appears in St. Peter's Square for first time in weeks
item.description: Pope Francis greeted crowds in St. Peter's Square on Sunday, his first public appearance since being discharged from the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel walks back its account of the killing of 15 medics in Gaza after video seems to contradict it
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Israeli military backtracked on its account of the killing of 15 Palestinian medics by its forces last month after phone video appeared to contradict its claims that their vehicles did not have emergency signals on when troops opened fire on them in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, WAFAA SHURAFA and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: The Who... not too old to rock
item.description: Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend lit up London in a rare, fiery performance for Teenage Cancer Trust — decades older, still unmistakably The Who.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis has made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and medical workers in his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed at least six people.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: In its 3-year-old war, Russia has detained what human rights activists say are thousands of Ukrainian civilians and has charged them with various crimes.
item.dc:creator: Dasha Litvinova And Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US revoking visas for South Sudanese passport holders
item.description: The United States is revoking visas for South Sudanese passport holders because the country’s transitional government has not accepted citizens who were expelled from the U.S., according to the Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Zelensky hits US Embassy over 'surprisingly disappointing' response to Russian strike
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky criticized the U.S. Embassy in Ukraine over its “surprisingly disappointing” response to Russia’s strike on the city of Kryvyi Rih, Zelensky’s hometown. Zelensky, in a lengthy Saturday post on the social platform X, accused the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Argentina's Milei doubles down on Trump bromance as the world reels from trade shock
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Europe vowed retaliation. China plotted tariffs of its own. Mexico scrambled to blunt the blow. But while the world's leaders were wringing their hands over President Donald Trump's announcement of sweeping tariffs on U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's latest tariffs could affect your wallet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump has unveiled his latest tariffs, and they could have significant implications for your wallet. Trump's sweeping new tariffs, on top of previous levies and retaliation worldwide, are expected to increase prices for everyday items.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and CORA LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will know in 'matter of weeks' if Russia is serious about peace or using 'delay tactic': Rubio
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. will know within weeks whether Russia is serious about a ceasefire with Ukraine or if it's time to rethink U.S. policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luxury cruise ship caught in 40-foot waves while traveling through Drake Passage
item.description: Passengers on Quark Expeditions' Ocean Explorer ship got rocked with waves up to 40 feet on a recent cruise, rocking those onboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Experts warn Iran’s nuclear double-talk designed to buy time, undermine US pressure
item.description: President Donald Trump is sending both carrots and sticks towards Iran, stepping up military assets in the Middle East while also signaling talks are still on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houthis shoot down 3rd US reaper drone as Trump administration continues daily airstrikes
item.description: The Houthis shot down another U.S. Reaper drone, the third one that has been destroyed by the terror organization since early March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol’s political rise was fast: the former star prosecutor clinched South Korea’s presidency only a year after he entered politics.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio demands NATO pony up, dismisses 'hysteria' over US role under Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO allies they must make hard choices and increase defense spending to 5% to bolster capabilities in face of emboldened adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Burma earthquake death toll rises to 3,085, humanitarian crisis deepens
item.description: More than 3,000 people have died from the 7.7 magnitude earthquake that struck Burma last week, which has sent the country into a deeper humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio arrives in Brussels for NATO talks amid unease over Trump’s agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived at in Brussels Thursday for talks that allies hope will shed light on the U.S.’ future plans in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli leader Netanyahu will visit Hungary, defying an international arrest warrant
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is scheduled to arrive in Hungary on Wednesday to meet with its nationalist prime minister despite an international arrest warrant for the Israeli leader over the war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli defense minister says military is expanding its operation in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday, as airstrikes on the southern city of Khan Younis killed 17 people overnight, according to hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
